extraction.one_line_summary: PETERS Nans won from the early 13-rider breakaway while BARDET Romain attacked late to gain 13 seconds and move to 4th overall despite an earlier crash.
tactical_patterns: BARDET recovered from crash on climb to attack final slopes, converting team stage win momentum into GC time gain.
YATES Adam chased BARDET's late move but sacrificed gap control to limit losses, revealing defensive yellow jersey tactics.
POWLESS Neilson fifth from breakaway shows American durability on Pyrenean stages, limiting GC losses after yesterday's mechanical.

rider_signals.PETERS Nans: positive: First notable win, emerged from 13-rider break on Pyrenean stage suggests Grand Tour breakthrough capability
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: Crashed on climb yet attacked final slopes to gain 13 seconds into 4th GC, showing resilience under adversity
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: Infiltrated 13-rider break and finished fifth, recovered seven minutes lost to mechanical previous day
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: Chased BARDET's late attack but couldn't prevent 13-second loss, defensive rather than offensive yellow jersey tactics
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: Lost time to drop to 13 seconds down, defending champion showing vulnerability in Pyrenees
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Abandoned Tour de France on Pyrenean stage, European champion unable to survive mountain terrain
